description_long: This sculpture features a seated figure, likely a Buddha, set against a pointed, leaf-shaped mandorla with intricate engravings. The figure is positioned on a rectangular base with four sturdy legs, giving the impression of a small throne or altar. The front of the mandorla is adorned with detailed line work, while the back displays two smaller, less detailed figures or motifs. The surface has a weathered bronze appearance with patches of green patina, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The overall form is compact and symmetrical, with the mandorla rising vertically behind the central figure. The craftsmanship emphasizes both the spiritual subject and the durability of the material.
description: A compact bronze sculpture of a seated figure, likely a Buddha, framed by a leaf-shaped mandorla and set on a four-legged base with green patina highlights.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin profile of the mandorla and the depth of the base.
description_view_1: Frontal view displaying the seated figure and detailed engravings on the mandorla.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again highlighting the slimness of the mandorla and the rectangular base.
description_view_3: Rear view revealing two smaller figures or motifs engraved on the back of the mandorla.
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the background is plain and does not indicate an environment.
